entry.title: Integrating SAP Datasphere with SAP Integrated Business Planning, using SAP CI - Part 2
entry.content:
To create a transparent and a feasible supply chain plan – one needs not only master data and transactional data, but we also need contextual data that might be available in multiple data sources across the IT landscape. SAP Datasphere - the next generation of SAP Data Warehouse cloud is a sophisticated tool to consolidate data from various sources and build a business data fabric that can be semantically modelled and fed to the state-of -the art supply chain planning solution – SAP IBP. In this blog I explain the steps I took to read data from a space in SAP Datasphere, transform this data and then write it to SAP IBP using SAP Cloud Integration.

Fig.1 End to end components used for this Integration.

Here is a recap of what we did so far:

  1. In this experiment, we want to read data from SAP IBP using web-socket RFC as a protocol in SAP Cloud Integration. Then we transform this data using XSLT and then use JDBC to write this data to SAP Datasphere. For this,
  2. We created a communication interface using the SAP_COM_0931 in SAP IBP. Based on which we also created a destination on the BTP.
  3. In SAP Datasphere, we created a space, user and a table called IBPDEMAND. Imagine this table has some data which we want to write back to SAP IBP.
  4. We use SAP Cloud Integration as our middleware. We created a JDBC data source alias which we used in our iFlows. 

Reading data from SAP Datasphere

In our previous blog, we did create our database user. We have the credentials which can use to read data using an Open SQL Statement. If you consider a simple SELECT statement, we can prepare an XML payload which we can send to the JDBC adapter as documented in this help. The following XML payload would help us do this.

<root>
        	<Select_Statement>
        		<dbTableName action="SELECT">
        			<table><xsl:value-of select="$SAPDSPTarget"/></table>
        			<access>
        			    	<PRODUCT/>
            				<CUSTOMER/>
            				<LOCATION/>
            				<CONSDEMAND/>
            				<KEYFIGUREDATE/>
            				<UNITS/>
            				<COMMENTS/>
        			</access>	
        			<!—
<key1><PRODUCT hasQuot="Yes">IBP-120</PRODUCT></key1>
        			<key2><CUSTOMER hasQuot="Yes">0017100002</CUSTOMER></key2>
 -->
        		</dbTableName>
        	</Select_Statement> 
        </root>

The above XSLT would generate a select statement from a table where the table name is a parameter $SAPDSPTarget. This table name can be either parameterized via configuration or passed to this iFlow as part of a payload. I exposed the man iFlow as a HTTP end point and passed a JSON payload in which the Datasphere target table name was a key value pair. The XSLT can be used in an iFlow like show below.

Fig.2. Reading data from SAP Datasphere. It uses a sub flow which is called via a Process direct.

In the above iFlow section, the Process direct calls a subflow which can be reused multiple times. The sub-flow uses the JDBC adapter to send the generated Open SQL command to SAP Datasphere. If there are records, they are represented in an XML payload as a response from the JDBC adapter in the following format:

<root><Select_Statement_response><row>…. Where, the row element is an array of records based on the select. A router is used to check if there were records fetched by the select statement. This response is stored in a temporary variable for further transformations. 

Writing to SAP IBP

Data is written to SAP IBP in an asynchronous manner. The following steps explain the process:

  1. At first a batch id is created to write data to IBP in batches.
  2. Data is initially stored in a staging area which is internal to SAP IBP.
  3. Once all data is stored in the staging area, then the post processing is started based on the batch id.
  4. The status for the post processing step is monitored for errors. These errors are relevant to the business context. For example, a wrong posting date, a miss matching product, customer, location relationship, invalid planning area, etc.
  5. If all data was process successful, the data will be available in the target planning area.                      

The screenshot below, shows the entire steps end to end in an iFlow.

Fig. 3 An IFlow in Cloud Integration which can read data from SAP Datasphere and write to SAP IBP asynchronously.

Once we receive the data from SAP Datapshere via the JDBC adapter, I used the following XSLT transformation to prepare a payload that is sent to the SAP IBP system to create a batch ID

<IBPWriteBatches>
<IBPWriteBatch  Key="${header.IBPBatchKey}" Name="SAP Datasphere Planning Data"
Destination="${header.IBPDestination}"
Command="INSERT_UPDATE"
PlanningArea="${header.IBPPlanningArea}"
PlanningAreaVersion=""/>
</IBPWriteBatches>

If the batch was created, I prepared the following payload to write this data from Datasphere to the staging tables.

<multimap:Messages>
           <multimap:Message1> 
               <IBPWriteKeyFigures>                   
                    <xsl:attribute name="FieldList"><xsl:value-of select="$IBPFields"></xsl:value-of></xsl:attribute>
           		    <xsl:attribute name="BatchKey"><xsl:value-of select="$IBPBatchKey"/></xsl:attribute>	
           		    <xsl:attribute name="FileName">SAPDatasphereDataLoad</xsl:attribute> 
                   
                   <xsl:variable name="InputPayload" select="parse-xml($DSPPayload)"/>                    
                   
                    <xsl:for-each select="$InputPayload/root/Select_Statement_response/row">
                       <item>
                           <PRDID><xsl:value-of select="./PRODUCT"></xsl:value-of></PRDID>
                           <LOCID><xsl:value-of select="./LOCATION"></xsl:value-of></LOCID>
                           <CUSTID><xsl:value-of select="./CUSTOMER"></xsl:value-of></CUSTID>
                           <CONSENSUSDEMANDQTY><xsl:value-of select="./CONSDEMAND"></xsl:value-of></CONSENSUSDEMANDQTY>
                           <KEYFIGUREDATE><xsl:value-of select="./KEYFIGUREDATE"></xsl:value-of></KEYFIGUREDATE>
                       </item>
                   </xsl:for-each> 
               </IBPWriteKeyFigures>
            </multimap:Message1>
    	</multimap:Messages>

Taking a closer look, you can see that a FieldList is first created. This list contains a coma separated string with the name of the Key figure and all its root attributes which are needed in IBP. The same list is also used to create the column values in the for-each loop where the mapping between the column name from the Datasphere table and the SAP IBP attributes and keyfigure is done. Once this payload is created, we can use the helper iFlows to send this data to the SAP IBP backend. I used the process direct called - SAP_IBP_Write_-_Post_Data which is implemented in the helper iFlows which internally uses the Web-socket RFC calls to send this payload to the SAP IBP backend.  

After all the records are written to SAP IBP, we can start the post processing step using the process direct - SAP_IBP_Write_-_Process_Posted_Data. This uses the batch id internally to call the respective web-socket RFC interface. Once the post processing step is completed a status message is sent back to the caller. If the post processing was successful, the XML response would look like,

<multimap:Message2>
      <IBPWriteBatch Command="INSERT_UPDATE"
                     CreatedAt="2024-03-04T14:16:43Z"                     CreatedBy="CC0000000083"
                     Destination="IBP_G5R_100_CLONING"                Id="2595"
                     Key="ConsensusDemand"                                       Name="SAP Datasphere Planning Data"
                     ProcessingEndedAt="2024-03-04T14:17:46Z"     ProcessingInProgress="false"
                     ProcessingStartedAt="2024-03-04T14:17:43Z"   ScheduleStatus="OK"
                     ScheduledAt="2024-03-04T14:16:43.521Z"         WorstProcessingStatus="PROCESSED">
         <Files>
            <File Count="2"
                  Name="SAPDatasphereDataLoad"                           PlanningArea="XPACNT2305"
                  Status="PROCESSED"                                                  TypeOfData="Key Figure Data"/>
         </Files>
         <Messages/>
         <ScheduleResponse>
            <Status>OK</Status>
            <Messages/>
         </ScheduleResponse>
      </IBPWriteBatch>
   </multimap:Message2>

Here you can interpret that key figure data was written to the IBP Destination name IBP_G5R_100_CLONING in a planning area called XPACNT2305 and the status of the post processing was OK.

Conclusion

In this blog, we showed the main aspects of how to read data from SAP Datasphere and write this to SAP IBP using the Cloud Integration as a middleware. SAP IBP is bundled with a middleware tool – SAP Cloud Integration for data services (CI-DS). In my next blog, I share how we can achieve the same steps using CI-DS.

entry.title: Re: Cleaning out Legacy Held Transactions to Improve Payout Runtime in Agent Performance Management
entry.content:
@PhillipButts 

Kindly remove the SAP internal portal link which is your blog APM Workzone hyperlink

entry.title: Re: How to get access to Client instance (Implementation)
entry.content:
If you are asking as a partner, unfortunately, the customer community is not for supporting partners with configuration questions. You can post questions into the partner community here:


https://groups.community.sap.com/t5/delivery-forums/ct-p/hcm-delivery-forums


In addition, please leverage the partner enablement tools listed here...

https://me.sap.com/notes/2116056/E

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Greetings all! I'm a fresh face in the SAP community, having recently retired from the U.S. Army after 22 years of service. Transitioning into civilian life, I'm enthusiastic about embarking on a new career path within the realm of SAP. I've observed numerous colleagues make strides in their careers after engaging with SAP programs, and I'm eager to follow suit. With a background in Engineering and Military Intelligence, I recognize the significance of SAP and its contributions to the DoD enterprise. I'm keen to contribute to and be a part of this dynamic environment.

entry.title: Re: Verify Course Completion
entry.content: This depends on how your SCORM content was published. It sounds like it sends a "complete course on launch" message to the lms. You can test the content @ cloud.scorm.com which will give you more details on what triggered the completion.

entry.title: SAP Brazilian User Group (ASUG Brazil) – SIG Localization Virtual Meeting
entry.content:
Join ASUG Brazil in their virtual meeting for Localization to learn about the latest updates in SAP solutions to address emerging regulations in Brazil. 

Register here 

This event is targeted at members of ASUG Brazil. The sessions will be conducted in Brazilian Portuguese.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am from New York. Currently calling Texas home by way of U.S. Army retirement. 🙂

entry.title: Re: #COE#RFL01-Withholding Tax not Calculated Automatical...
entry.content:
Hello Mouna, issue is it is not calculating base amount so even after maintaining tax rate tax value is zero. Ideally base amount should be gross or net value based on withholding tax type (HO) - just check once in withholding tax type setting under base right radio button of gross or net selected + under accumulation type no accumulation radio button selected.

Regards,

Sumesh Gupta

entry.title: Re: Depreciation Beyond Useful Life of an Asset
entry.content:
Hi @dublin Please check below thread. hope this helps.

https://gad5158842f.us2.hana.ondemand.com/dtp/viewer/#/tree/1670/actions/21661/?version=current
 
Regards,
Sumesh Gupta

entry.title: Error "Resource not found for the segment 'mergeId'" in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am working on SAP S4 HANA Cloud Public Edition version 2402, and I am reaching you for help regarding an error that is displayed when I try to access Fiori application "Manage launchpad spaces".

The error text: "Resource not found for the segment 'mergeId'"

I checked first if this is related to an authorization issue, but this is not the case because both business catalog "SAP_CORE_BC_UI_FLD" and business role "SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR" are assigned to my user. Also, I can successfully open fiori application "Manage Launchpad pages" (which requires the same "authorizations" as the fiori app "Manage Launchpad spaces") 

  • Thus, since I can successfully access fiori application "Manage Launchpad pages" but not able to call "Manage Launchpad spaces", I do not think this is an authorization issue.

I called fiori application "Odata Error log" and I see the following details:

  • Error: "Resource not found for the segment 'mergeId'
  • Service name: FDM_SPACE_REPOSITORY_CUST_SRV
  • ABAP Component: CA-FLP-ABA
  • ABAP Package: /IWCOR/SODATA_CORE
  • Request URI: /sap/opu/odata/UI2/FDM_SPACE_REPOSITORY_CUST_SRV/$batch?sap-client=100

What can I do to correct the issue ?

Should I contact SAP since this is the cloud Public Edition and I have no access to the gateway?

Also the SAP Note "1797736" did not help

entry.title: Issue Filtering on Incentive Management OData APIs (Kubernetes) PipelineRuns API
entry.content:
I'm trying to execute a GET request to retrieve PipelineRuns from the API in the subject.

In Postman, if I do a straight GET without any filters, I get 10 records back.  There are 147 rows in the CS_PIPELINERUN table for this environment as I've queried the table in WEBIDE.

I would like to be able to filter on the StartTime field of the API but I can't seem to be able to get the syntax correct.  As an example, if I want to retrieve just the one PipelineRun for March 29, 2023 at 19:05:35.480, I've tried this:  cng-pipeline/odata/PipelineRuns?$filter=StartTime eq 2023-03-29T19:05:35.480Z

This still brings back 10 records.  I then tried this (this is the actual value from the table):

cng-pipeline/odata/PipelineRuns?StartTime=2023-03-29 19:05:35.480000000

This also returns 10 records.

My questions are: 1. What is the correct syntax for filtering a PipelineRun using the StartTime?

2.  Is there some sort of limit on the number of records this API will allow to be returned and if so, how do I change/override it with a parameter?

In the documentation for the API, it says the Contact is the Incentive Management API Support Team but I can't seem to find anything about them, much less how to contact them.

I know I'm probably whistling up a tree but I'm really hoping somebody knows the answers to these questions and can help me.

Thanks in advance!

Ron

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: @JamelJ great to meet you in the community. I live outside of Philadelphia. My role at SAP is a community advocate. I would like to you to meet some folks who live near you @rileyrainey who engages with developers, @SergioG_TX who is SAP Champion and guru in many SAP product areas, and @DiegoDora  who is a SAP Mentor with deep focus on BTP among other techs. Best of luck!

entry.title: how to grey out "export" in SAC
entry.content: how to grey out "export" link like "Remove Reference" link.

entry.title: Re: The SAP Community is all moved in!
entry.content:
Thanks for the response Jerry. It's good to know new pages will have expandable images.

But I don't understand what you mean by "due to issues with the html". Did SAP not run conversion programs on the existing pages?

Did they do any testing on the conversion programs prior to release?

I'm sorry but I can't imagine any project committee approve a release when defects like this exist. It's a little unbelievable, especially from a company that keeps promoting user-centric, Design Thinking delivery methods. Did they forget their own methodology?

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: @KatjaSnyders it is great to meet you. I live in the US outside of Philadelphia. I want to introduce you to some other gurus in your field - @ChrisPaine and @DanielleLarocca who are both SAP Mentors. Also, I want you to meet @MattHarding who is a SAP Mentor who lives in Tasmania. Maybe you know each other already. Best of luck in your projects. You may have already started engaging with community members with similar interests. Human Capital Management | SAP Community

entry.title: Plant with split valuation and usage of future price
entry.content:
Hi

We are using a plant with split valuation but i didn't manage to input any future price (costing view doesn't seem to be available)

Is it an issue or am i doing something wrong ?

When dealing with a plant without split valuation, load of future price is fine

Thanks

Stéphane

entry.title: Re: 🇩🇪 Hands-on with the btp CLI and APIs (Frankenthal, DE)
entry.content:
Hi @Kaustavsap2 

in general your employer must be a DSAG member to attend this event. I checked with the DSAG colleagues though and they can make an exception.

Be aware that the codejam tomorrow is just a mini codejam from 1pm -3 pm.

The codejam on Thursday is a normal one.

entry.title: Re: Best way to generate Microsoft Word docx from ABAP
entry.content:
yes. you can do that. For example you have next template.

Ctrl+a, repeating control

 

name it

 

if you want each doc from new page then insert "page break" inside

entry.title: Using XCO, how do I get a list of sub packages for a given package?
entry.content:
I am attempting to use XCO classes to get a list of sub packages for a given package. I have working code to retrieve other things from a package (such as Data Definitions), but the comparable coding for packages is not behaving the same way. See the example coding here:

    DATA(lo_package) = xco_cp_abap_repository=>package->for( 'ZMD_LOCAL' ).

    DATA(lo_entities_name)   = xco_cp_abap_sql=>constraint->contains_pattern( 'ZMD_I_%' ).
    DATA(lo_entities_filter) = xco_cp_abap_repository=>object_name->get_filter( lo_entities_name ).
    DATA(lo_entities)        = xco_cp_abap_repository=>objects->ddls->where( it_filters = VALUE #( ( lo_entities_filter ) ) ).
    DATA(lt_entities)        = lo_entities->in( lo_package )->get( ).
" at this point, lt_entities has the correct content

    DATA(lo_packages_name)   = xco_cp_abap_sql=>constraint->contains_pattern( 'ZMD%' ).
    DATA(lo_packages_filter) = xco_cp_abap_repository=>object_name->get_filter( lo_packages_name ).
    DATA(lo_packages)        = xco_cp_abap_repository=>objects->devc->where( it_filters = VALUE #( ( lo_packages_filter ) ) ).
    DATA(lt_packages)        = lo_packages->in( lo_package )->get( ).
" same logic, but lt_packages only contains one entry for the superpackage that was provided above, ZMD_LOCAL instead of an entry for each of its subpackages

Any hints on how to go about getting the sub packages?

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements app shows wrong decimal separators
entry.content: Finally, I found out this is a simple BUG in the SAPUI5 version 1.108.22, according to Note 3383359 - SAPUI5 Framework 1.108.22 wrong amount displayed

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA VBRK/VBRP new field DRAFT
entry.content:
It's that old weird behavior of LEFT JOIN on a table with INNER JOIN which makes the inner join behave like a left join.

(in your case, VBPA)

entry.title: How to create the variant with dynamic selections using abap program
entry.content:
Hi, 

Can anybody help me to create the variant for fbl1n using abap program by passing the multiple values for document number in dynamic selections screen . It will be helpful if you provide the sample code.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: WebUI in ChaRM - How to change field label?
entry.content:
Does anybody know the solution?

That would be very helpful for me.

Thank you.

entry.title: How to make reserved material take to own stock if activity is closed but service call is open?
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Base of Project: FSM with C4C , C4C with S4 HANA as ERP

We are using Consignment fill up order & Pick up order, as reserved material in FSM.

For Open Service Call with Open Activity we can do Take to Own Stock & Use of material

For Open Service Call with Closed Activity i am not able to do Take to Own stock , Showing as "Moving 1 Material failed, because they have a quantity of Zero"

 

 

Some times engineer is missed to select take to own stock in activity so its should available to take to own stock if engineer is responsible for that ticket.

Thanks & Regards

entry.title: Re: Using Ariba APIs to extract Ariba contract data
entry.content:
1) You have to register in api.ariba.com

2) Request "Strategic Contract Workspaces" API Access; It will take about a day for them to approve.

3) Generate "Oauth Secret"

4) Generate Bearr token using info in step 3

5) Following call will get you all contract data; you have to replace "user" and "realm" parameters with your environment specific values.

 

 

https://openapi.ariba.com/api/retrieve-contract-workspaces/v1/prod//contractWorkspaces?user=User_id_with_contract_access&passwordAdapter=PasswordAdapter1&realm=your_realm_name

entry.title: Re: Not opening BRF+ application
entry.content: Did you find any solution for this.

entry.title: Variable Pay Template Individual Rating source
entry.content:
Hi there, when I set a rating source for Variable Pay Template Individual Rating I have to set, which Goal Section I'd like to choose as Rating. However, when launching the VPY Worksheet, it uses the other Goal Section withing the PM Form. The issue exist also the other way around. Any ideas why this might happen?

Thanks a lot

entry.title: How to use only PGI items in FSM reserved material ?
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Base of Project : FSM with C4C, C4C with S4HANA

Using: Consignment fill Up & Pick Order for Part as reserved material in FSM

Issue: All added parts in C4C is coming in FSM which results in confusion in Engineer & its creating loophole in Consumption & Pick Up orders.

We know ATP confirmed items can be release to FSM from C4C but its still open loophole that Actual PGI part is not coming for usage.

Thanks & Regards

entry.title: Hide Save Button (BTP, RAP)
entry.content:
Hi experts! 

is it possible to Hide the Save Button on the right bottom corner on the object page?

I'm using RAP on BTP. A change in the manifest.json or so would also be fine.

Thanks, Sebastian

entry.title: Re: GRC Tuesdays: Announcing SAP’s plans for a next generation Governance, Risk, and Compliance Plat
entry.content: Hi Thomas - what should I look for on the PAM to show the upgrade path from SAP GRC version 12.0 to SAP GRC edition for SAP HANA?  As of now, it just shows the EOM as 31.12.2027 and no successor product versions.  The details are WIP, but the information on the PAM would clarify the promotion.  Thank you.

entry.title: Re: What is the difference between Ariba and Ariba Network?
entry.content:
This question was answered here

https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-q-a/differences-between-ariba-and-s4hana/qaq-p/11882244

entry.title: Navigating Career Transition to SAP
entry.content:
Transitioning careers can be daunting especially when navigating through different industries. Whether you' re experienced or starting out, the process presents unique challenges. I would like to open up discussion with current employees with SAP/community to share tips, advice on overcoming barriers to thrive in my career transition journey.  One common hurdle is I possess the expertise in business processes but my goal is to transition industries. How can I leverage my existing competencies, and accomplishments to excel in new industry within SAP ecosystem?

a. Effectively communicate transferable skills during the application resume process? Talent profile?

b. Specific resources or training programs you would recommend to not be passed over due to upskilling to a new industry?

I look forward to the collective wisdom and experience to help achieve success transitioning into a career with SAP.

entry.title: Re: Question on CDS Authorization for SAP delivered C_Fin...
entry.content:
access control (for embedded analytics)is done at cube level I_ActualPlanLineItemSemTag

query level don't need dcl, now you have one, but it is wide open,

entry.title: Re: Issue Filtering on Incentive Management OData APIs (K...
entry.content: @ronhagan report your issue to SAP Support team via ticket and show the screenshot with API response you get

entry.title: Re: Adding a Text field to an ABAP CDS View via extension.
entry.content: how about add the text field there, _MaterialText.YOURTEXTFIELD

entry.title: Restrict web based data maintenance tool for characteristic based on one compound object
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

   I created a characteristic that compounds two objects. One of the objects is an authorization relevant object that I assumed would automatically restrict data any where the new characteristic is used. When trying to populate the new characteristic using the web based master data maintenance tool, I noticed the data was not being restricted. What settings are necessary to make this possible?

I tried setting the Auth Relevant flag which did not help:

I tried the master data maintenance flag, but that stopped all data from showing. I assume security has to be setup for the combination of both compound objects in this case?

One setting I thought may help that I see in BW doesn't appear to be available in the Eclipse modeling tool.

Any help is appreciated. Thanks,

entry.title: Reaching shipment cost (LE-TRA) processing via Fiori?
entry.content:
Hello,

We can not find the relevant fiori apps to create shipment costs. Are these apps can not be reachable via Fiori? I can reach the t-codes via Gui. I already search in Fiori library but could not find the apps. Additionally I can reach the VT* tcodes via fiori. Why I can not find these t-codes? Is it because LE-TRA is not in the feature scope of S/4 Hana? Thank you.

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.05
entry.content:
Hi @Koji_Y , 

Sharing response from product management:

Excel desktop (on Mac) prohibits cross-domain calls between SAC and source system (BW/S4). So the only option we have is tunnel connection – direct connection does not work. This is a technical limitation for Excel desktop.

entry.title: migração Appgyver para SAP BUILD
entry.content:
Não consegui fazer a migração AppGyver Community para nova plataforma SAP BUILD para data 31/01/2024. Não consigo recuperar senha, logar, entrar, na antiga plataforma e exportar para importar meu projeto no SAP BUILD. O suporte consegue me recuperar projeto ReceitasPaganini please, socorro!

Receitas Paganini

entry.title: Re: Explaining SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to a Beginner
entry.content:
Is this four products of sap are live in sap ask to peer expert channel if yes only then I will apply to be an chat expert in SAP for ask to peer expert channel for any of this one category between 4,

 

SAP Fiori Launchpad

 

SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform

 

SAP Crystal Reports

 

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Re: Termination Date FMS to get End Date if Status is Ter...
entry.content:
Try this, set to refresh when field changes Agreement Status

SELECT CASE WHEN $[$1250000036.0.0] = 'T' THEN $[$1250000016.0.0] ELSE $[$1250000022.0.0] END FROM DUMMY

entry.title: Re: How to create materials with different codes ?
entry.content:
Probably makes more sense to learn the menu paths over transaction codes.

E.g. from the help pages:

Material > Create > (general) > Immediately  

2 help doc links:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/7b24a64d9d0941bda1afa753263d9e39/1f8bc95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/f7fddfe4caca43dd967ac4c9ce6a70e4/36528c53f894ee23e10000000a174cb4.html

entry.title: Re: How to connect Service layer with angular,
entry.content: Pudistes solucionarlo estoy en el mismo problema intentando conectar con angular al services layer de sap

entry.title: Asset Under Construction
entry.content:
Hi All ,

I have hundreds of open project & need to find out associated asset under construction & asset list for these open projects . Also how much amount is settled to these AUC & Asset till current date & what is their current value ? Can anyone please shed some light how can I find these data ? 

Thanking you very much .

Best Regards 

Mohammed Mehebub Rahman

entry.title: Re: Adobe Forms: Logo based on Print Langauge
entry.content:
This may or may not be relevant, as I am unsure of the context of your forms.  This answer relates to ABAP driven forms.

Save each of your logos under transaction code SE78, giving them a name that includes the word LOGO (for later easier identification of which images represent logos compared to other images), also include the country in the name, and a version number, as you may decide to change logos at some point in the future.

Once saved, these can then be referenced in your forms Interface.  create a global variable of type XSTRINGVAL to hold the logo image.  Next check your current language and set a local variable to your logo name to reference the relevant logo for that language that you saved in SE78.   Use this variable to call method cl_ssf_xsf_utilities=>get_bds_graphic_as_bmp.  Look at examples of use of this on your system to see how it works.  This is used to load the actual returned graphic image into your global variable for the logo.

TIP: In your interface it is best not to reference the logo name direct, but instead have a lookup table on your database that has the form name in one column, the language in another column, and the logo name (created in SE78) in a third column.  In this way you can update the logo name in the table when you change versions, and not have to change the form interface each time.  If your forms are likely to have several images, create a fourth column called image type, which might have values like "Logo", "Map", "illustration", etc, that further identifies what the image is about in relation to the form.

In your form's context dialogue, you create the logo variable by right clicking and selecting Create >> Graphic

Name your graphic "LOGO" for ease of recognition.  Then in the Properties, associate this logo with the image global variable from your interface.

In the illustration I have used two fields from my interface, one that contains the name of the logo as saved under transaction SE78, and the other holding the logo MIME type.  

Once done, open your form in the designer and place the LOGO field where you want the logo to appear and size it appropriately.

All going well, when you run the form generation your driver program will check the language, retrieve the appropriate logo from SE78 and display it in the place you have placed the logo on the form.

entry.title: Continuous Improvement Culture: Fostering BPM Innovation
entry.content:
Continuous Improvement Culture: Fostering BPM Innovation

Photo by Diego PH on Unsplash

Continuous improvement is not just a concept; it’s a culture that organizations need to imbibe if they wish to stay ahead. Companies that embrace this philosophy not only stay ahead, but also thrive in an environment of innovation and adaptability. Within this framework, Business Process Management (BPM) plays a pivotal role, serving as a driver for efficiency, agility, and innovation across all facets of the organization.

Let’s delve into how organizations can cultivate a robust continuous improvement culture, and the role analysts, managers, and executives play in facilitating the process.

Before we do that however, it’s important to define what continuous improvement is and what is involved.

Understanding Continuous Improvement

Continuous improvement is not merely a set of procedures or methodologies; it’s a mindset ingrained within the organizational DNA. It entails constantly seeking ways to enhance processes, products, and services to deliver greater value to customers and stakeholders. At its core, continuous improvement will involve:

· Iterative Approach: Embracing a cycle of planning, implementing, evaluating, and refining processes to drive incremental enhancements.

· Empowerment and Collaboration: Encouraging employees at all levels to contribute ideas and actively participate in the improvement process, fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation.

· Data-Driven Decision Making: Leveraging data analytics and insights to identify areas for improvement and measure the impact of changes effectively.

Now that we know what it means, lets discuss how each role plays a part.

For Analysts: Experiment and Evaluate

Analysts are critical to every organization’s potential for innovation. They are the bridge between technology and business, with the essential duty of evaluating existing business procedures, identifying areas of improvement, and developing strategic plans for enhancement.

An effective way analysts can boost BPM innovation is by adopting a proactive approach toward business processes. This includes implementing diverse tools, technologies, and methodologies to experiment with new strategies and evaluate their outcomes. By doing so, they can identify the most efficient ways to enhance business processes and make informed recommendations to the organization.

Moreover, analysts should continuously scan the business environment to stay updated on the latest trends, leverage them in their analyses, and keep innovation at the forefront.

What tools can they use:

1. Process Mapping and Analysis: Analysts play a crucial role in identifying inefficiencies and bottlenecks within existing processes. By conducting thorough process mapping exercises and root cause analyses, they lay the groundwork for improvement initiatives.

2. Kaizen Events: Engaging in Kaizen events or rapid improvement workshops allows analysts to collaborate cross-functionally, brainstorm solutions, and implement changes quickly to address pressing issues.

3. Training and Skill Development: Providing analysts with ongoing training in process improvement methodologies, such as Lean Six Sigma, equips them with the tools and knowledge to drive meaningful change.

For Managers: Nurture and Motivate

Managers play a vital role in inculcating the culture of continuous improvement by nurturing innovative thinkers and motivating them to explore novel solutions. They act as the catalysts who accelerate the application of analytical findings into practical process enhancements.

Managers can foster a culture of innovation by creating an open and collaborative environment where ideas are welcomed, experimented, and implemented. They should encourage their teams to approach problems creatively, make mistakes, and learn from them.

Moreover, they should ensure streamlined communication between analysts and the execution team, acknowledging even small achievements, and setting realistic expectations. All these combined will continually trigger innovation and enhance overall efficiency.

Managers Summary:

1. Set Clear Goals and Objectives: Managers need to articulate the organization’s vision for continuous improvement and align it with strategic goals. Clear objectives provide a roadmap for prioritizing improvement initiatives and measuring success.

2. Foster a Supportive Environment: Creating a culture where experimentation and learning from failure are encouraged empowers teams to take calculated risks and innovate without fear of repercussion.

3. Recognition and Rewards: Acknowledging and rewarding employees for their contributions to continuous improvement initiatives reinforces desired behaviors and motivates teams to actively participate in the process.

For Executives: Lead and Inspire

Continuous improvement and innovation culture starts at the top. As the stewards of strategic direction, executives play a pivotal role in setting the tone for innovation.

Executives need to lead by example, demonstrating their commitment to continuous improvement through active participation. This includes regularly communicating the importance of advancement, aligning innovation strategies with the overall business vision, and taking actionable steps to achieve them.

Additionally, executives must inspire employees across all levels. This could be achieved through recognizing contributors, providing them with the right resources, and above all, creating a safe space for experimentation and learning.

Executive Summary

1. Leadership Commitment: Executives must champion the cause of continuous improvement from the top down, demonstrating unwavering commitment and allocating resources to support improvement efforts.

2. Investment in Technology and Infrastructure: Providing access to cutting-edge tools and technology accelerates the pace of improvement initiatives and enables organizations to stay ahead in a rapidly evolving landscape.

3. Culture of Transparency and Communication: Executives play a pivotal role in fostering open communication channels where feedback is valued, and ideas are welcomed from all levels of the organization.

Activities for Driving BPM Innovation — All Levels

Cultivating a continuous improvement culture requires a systematic approach, participation from all levels of the organization, and a steadfast commitment to innovation.

Where does BPM fit in and how can it be used?

· Cross-Functional Collaboration Workshops: Bring together stakeholders from different departments and levels to identify pain points, brainstorm solutions, and design optimized processes collaboratively.

· Process Automation and Digitization: Leverage BPM tools and technologies to streamline workflows, eliminate manual tasks, and enhance operational efficiency.

· Continuous Monitoring and Feedback Loops: Implement systems for real-time monitoring of key performance indicators (KPIs) with managers and executives and gather feedback from end-users to identify areas for further optimization.

· Pilot Projects and Experimentation: Executives and managers, encourage teams to run pilot projects to test new ideas and innovations on a small scale before scaling them up across the organization.

· Knowledge Sharing and Learning Sessions: Don’t keep the learnings to yourself. Facilitate regular knowledge sharing sessions, workshops, and training programs to disseminate best practices and foster a culture of continuous learning.

In Summary

As we have discussed, cultivating a culture of continuous improvement requires a concerted effort from all levels of the organization, with analysts, managers, and executives each playing a distinct yet interconnected role. By embracing this ethos and leveraging BPM as a catalyst for innovation, organizations can drive sustainable growth, enhance customer satisfaction, and stay ahead in an increasingly competitive marketplace.

— — — — — — — — — — — -

If you found this article helpful, be sure to click like. And course follow me for more on the topics of AI, BPM, Process Mining, Customer Experience, Digital Transformation, and Automation.

Until next time — Keep Transforming 💡

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 3
entry.content:
const { CloudEvent, HTTP } = require("cloudevents");
require('dotenv').config();

//cloud event data
const type = "com.awesome_company.sales_order.Updated.v1"
const source = "https://prod.illidian.awesome_company.com/sales_orders" 
const salesOrderID = "9024000013"
const company = "AWO01"
const year = 2024
const statusSO = "Shipped"

const subject = company + salesOrderID + year

const data = {
    "salesOrder": salesOrderID,
    "company": company,
    "year": year,
    "status": statusSO
}

const sapcommunityid = "xavisanse"
//creating event

const soEvent = new CloudEvent({type,source,subject,data,sapcommunityid})


//sending event to Solace
const solace_protocol = "https"
const solace_topic = `dev-challenge/week-3/${sapcommunityid}/ce`

const { headers, body } = HTTP.structured(soEvent);

const postURL = `${solace_protocol}://${process.env.SOLACE_HOST}:${process.env.SOLACE_PORT}/TOPIC/${solace_topic}`

// Basic Authentication
const basicAuth = 'Basic ' + Buffer.from(process.env.SOLACE_USER + ':' + process.env.SOLACE_PWD).toString('base64');

headers["Authorization"] = basicAuth;

console.log("### March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 3 ###\n");
console.log("- HTTP Request Event Headers - \n")
for (const key in headers) {
    console.log(`Key: "${key}", Value: "${headers[key]}"`);
  }

console.log("\n - HTTP Request Event Body - \n");
console.log(JSON.stringify(JSON.parse(body), null, 1) + "\n");
console.log("\n - HTTP Response - ")

// Sending POST request to Solace
fetch(postURL, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: headers,
    body: body
})
.then(response => {
    // Check if the response is OK
    if (!response.ok) {
        throw new Error('Network response was not ok');
    }
    // Return a response with HTTP status and a message
    return {
        status: response.status,
        message: 'CloudEvent successfully sent to Solace.'
    };
})
.then(responseObj => {
    // Print the response
    console.log('Response:', responseObj);
})
.catch(error => {
    // Print error message
    console.error('There was a problem with the fetch operation:', error);
});

Screenshot of the call: 

url of the log: https://ce-dev-challenge-wk3.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/consumed-messages/webapp/index.html#/messages(08ee690b-6751-4b5f-88f3-5ba584e5c786)

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Integration Suite - New Message Metrics
entry.content:
What about Process Direct. That is not counted or is that wrong.

Iflow 1) send a sync message to iflow 2)  via Process Direct. Iflow 2 then sends the message via HTTP. 

 

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Power BI Template Connection
entry.content:
Hi Natalie,

It's hard to know what is causing the issue without more information, but two options are:

1. Something on your network is blocking the communication between Power BI and your Manager server.  You can get your IT team to take a look and see if they can identify an issue
2. You are not turned on tracking in SAP Companion so there is no data to display in the dashboard.  Make sure that you have set the following parameter in your SAP Companion configuration:

trackingUrlSEN=https://[companyname].enable-now.cloud.sap/wa/[WorkareaID]/

You need to replace [companyname] with the name from your SAP Enable Now URL and [WorkareaID] with the ID of the Workarea that contains your SAP Companion content.

Hope that helps.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: SF Employee Central Contingent Worker End Date error
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP has agreed to resolve the defect as the issue is reported by other clients as well. We are awaiting fix from SAP.

Thank you!

entry.title: How to enable an OData V4 service for anonymous access?
entry.content:
Introduction

This requirement stems from a customer that asked how to publish an OData V4 service in an SAP S/4HANA on premise system such that it could be used on a public web site without the need to provide any authentication.

With OData V2 this requirement can be achieved more easily since here every service has it's own SICF node where it would be possible to store credentials just for this service.

In OData V4 we have only one SICF node, namely /sap/opu/odata4/.

This is obviously a problem since there is no service specific node available in SICF.

Solution

The problem can be solved using the following approach.

  1. Create a role that only contains the S_START authorization for the one single OData V4 service we want to publish , 
    help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_nw75/7.5.5/de-DE/c6/dd838722ce4b8d9cc4a0741d93d864/frameset.htm
  2. Create a technical user that has only the authorization to access the single OData V4 service we want to make accessible.
  3. Create an alias in SICF for the node /sap/opu/odata4/ 
  4. Store the credentials of the aforementioned user in the alias
  5. Test the service  

Result

As we can see accessing the service zrap630ui_shop_o4_05a via the alias zodatav4 works.

https://10.79.21.221:44301/sap/opu/zodatav4/sap/zrap630ui_shop_o4_05a/srvd/sap/zrap630ui_shop_o4_05a/0001/?sap-client=100

Whereas accessing a second OData V4 service zrap630ui_shop_o4_05b via the alias zodatav4 does NOT work

This has been achieved by assigne the follwoing role the technical user which only contains the authorization to start the first service based on the authorization object S_START.

Please note:
Since OData V4 service use the authorization object S_START which is based on the service name it would for example be possible to publish several services that are in the same name range like

zrap630ui_shop_o4_05A, zrap630ui_shop_o4_05B, ... zrap630ui_shop_o4_05Z,  

How to section

Create the role

Using PFCG and the role template /IWBEP/RT_MGW_USR we create a role as shown in the screen shot above that only contains the S_START authorization of one OData V4 service called ZRAP630UI_SHOP_O4_05A.

Create the user

As a user we create a technical user whose credentials will be stored in the system alias.

Create the system alias

1. We start by right-clicking on the node opu and choose New subelement.

2. We create a new service element called ZODATAV4_2 and choose the option Alias to an existing service.

3. We choose the tab Target, select the node OdataV4 by double-clicking on it.

4. We navigate to the tab Logon Data, choose Alternative Logon Procedure and enter the credentials of our service user.

5. We navigate down and remove all logon procedures beside Logon using service data.

6. Do not forget to activate the link in SICF.

7. Check the result

entry.title: Is it possible to change the format of a dynamic parameter?
entry.content:
I am using a parameter that refers to a field that returns the options as:

which is the year month day in a decimal format.  Is there a way to convert these to month day year or at least in a more user friendly fashion?

entry.title: plateau report designer Resource explorer missing
entry.content:
Hi,
I am designing a report using PRD. one of the steps as per a guide is to add report parameters through 'Resources Explorer. I don't see this tab. How do I enable this tab ?
Thank you

entry.title: Different SAP Data Services versions for UAT and PROD environment feasibility
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Current we are using a separate Product key for SAP Data Services for UAT and PROD environment. As we have SAP data service 4.2 for PROD and SAP data service 4.3 for UAT .Due to the downtime complications, we are unable to upgrade our PROD instance to SAP data service 4.3

We want to verify if it is feasible to keep two different Product Keys one for UAT testing and other for PROD environment.

Also, confirm even if the same is feasible, there will be no compliance issue for the same.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Different SAP Data Services versions for UAT and PROD...
entry.content:
The recommended way is to have both your lower environment and production environment on the exact same versions of the applications installed.

If you dont have same version, then in case if you have any issues happening in production environment , we cannot test it in your lower environment first and we will need to directly modify the production environment for eg. if the fix is released in the patch upgrade then you would need to directly apply the patch upgrade without testing it in lower environment first which may have unforeseen issues.

Also, Data Services 4.2 has End of Mainstream Support in May 31, 2023 and End of Priority 1 Support in Dec 2024. Which means by from next year , if any issues happen in your production environment, you will not be able to create a P1 ticket for your issue. Note- You can still create P2, P3, P4 priority ticket.

There is no other compliance issue for having different versions/product keys of Data Services.

entry.title: Re: UI Configuration for Standard Validation Rules - H1 2021
entry.content:
Hi @FabriceB ,

No unfortunately the rules configured will apply to both the worksheets and Executive Review.

Thanks,

Christian

entry.title: Job and Automation Monitoring features - SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM is an out-of-box, native cloud solution which serves as the central hub for managing SAP landscapes, offering guided implementation driven by content and highly automated operations. In this blog post, I aim to delve into a very crucial capability of cloud ALM: Job and Automation Monitoring.

Through Job and Automation monitoring within SAP Cloud ALM, it is possible to monitor SAP S/4 HANA Application jobs, SAP ABAP jobs, SAP BW Process Chains, SAP Build Process Automation Jobs(or SAP Intelligent RPA jobs) and SAP Business workflow and Job scheduler service jobs in customer BTP application.

Job & Automation Monitoring guarantees uninterrupted business operations while enhancing the quality and performance of business process execution. This is achieved through:

  • Monitoring the health of job executions and detecting anomalies during execution.
  • Promptly alerting both business and IT users about disruptions and exceptions.
  • Providing detailed insights at the job execution level to facilitate fact-based root cause analysis.
  • Minimizing configuration efforts by leveraging historical execution information.

 

Having recently implemented Job and Automation Monitoring, I'm excited to outline its standout features that make it exceptionally useful.

  1. Overview – Overview provides a summary status regarding job execution for the Managed Components. It shows the status of the latest execution of every job with regards to the Technical exceptions, Application exceptions and run time. It also displays the number of unconfirmed alerts and status events.

   

Clicking on a card takes us to the Monitoring view which shows the list of jobs and automations with their latest Execution status, Application Status, Start delay and runtime.

Rating rules are visible in Configuration section under Monitoring Rating Rules. Execution Status and Application Status are non-modifiable, but Start Delay and Run Time can be modified as per requirement.

 

Clicking on the job name allows to further drill down to the list of executions of the selected job. Additionally, clicking on the information icon provides further details and enables direct navigation to the job within the managed component by selecting the Run ID.

 

2. Data collection – Data collection offers a convenient feature to toggle the Collection of data for managed Objects. Within the configuration section, under Managed components, there is an option to Switch On or Off the data collection for managed components.

 

3. Heart Beat Status – This feature visualizes the heartbeat status of managed services. Within the configuration section, under Managed Components, users can view the heartbeat status. If there is no data collection, a red-light indicator will be displayed.

 

4. Alerting – Alerting in cloud ALM allows to configure alerts and notifications based on the status of job executions within the managed components. Alerts can be set up for various events, such as execution status, application status, runtime, or start delay, ensuring timely awareness and proactive management of the system.

 

Event Name in the alert can be adjusted to indicate the type of the alert for which event has been created.

 

Execution status rating for the event can also be chosen while setting up the event.

 

There is another recently introduced feature “At Every Occurrence”. Normally, consecutive job failures prompt just one notification until the alert is acknowledged. However, this new option allows users to receive alerts for each individual instance of job failure.

 

Recipients for email notifications can be maintained in Event Actions under “Send email to” section. Email IDs need to be maintained in the “Notification Management” application.

 

Job and automation monitoring deliver an enriched notification email:

  • Subject Line: The event name is augmented with the corresponding job name.
  • Content: Detailed information regarding the failed job execution is provided. Additionally, the email body contains a direct hyperlink to the alert, facilitating immediate access to the alert within Cloud ALM.

 

5. Integration with Business Service Management(BSM) – Within the Business Service Management application in SAP Cloud ALM for operations, we can see business services, manage assigned cloud services and technical systems, and handle associated events. To view status events such as maintenance, disruption, upgrade etc. In the homepage Overview tab, there is an option to view the status of event for the managed components. Events are maintained in Business Service Management application.

 

6. Analytics – The Analysis view allows users to pinpoint jobs with extended response times and highest number of exceptions. For any job it shows aggregated information on total and average run time, number of executions and number of failed executions and the resulting failure rate. By clicking on the arrow at the end of every line, we can drill down into various trend charts for the metrics.

 

Hope this blog post helps you in understanding the various features of Job and Automation monitoring in cloud ALM.

Please share your feedback and queries.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP hybrid testing getting 'ECONNRESET' error whe...
entry.content: hey shahbhat, have you been able to resolve this problem?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to change the format of a dynamic para...
entry.content:
Hi David5111, there are different options for this depending on what you want the prompt to look like.

If you want to use a calendar based prompt:

  1. On your report create a new Parameter which has a Date Type.
  2. Then in your Record Selection Formula add something like the following.
{your date field} = year({?your date param})*10000+month({?your date param})*100+day({?your date param})

entry.title: Re: Create an xP&A Business Flow with SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Datasphere & SAP Cloud Integr
entry.content:
Hi @tcayzergues,

What a great blog post! Thanks for sharing. There are many things: Google Cloud Platform SFTP, SAP Integration Suite, SAP Datasphere, SAP Datasphere Open SQL Scheme (HANA Cloud), SAP Analytics Cloud. 

I'm wondering if Generic SFTP connection in SAP Datasphere could be used directly then it could allow to save data directly to a local table in SAP Datasphere. 

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Re: BAPI_SYSTEM_ALERT_GETDETAILS

Hello, 

I am facing same issue with SRM monitor, did u solved this issue ? can u please share the solution ?

Kind Regards

entry.title: Negative budget transfer restriction in funds management
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using FM-BCS in S/4 HANA 2021.We are using 7 dimensions(Fund, Fund center, Commitment item, Funded program, Budget period, functional area and grant)for budget posting and actuals.

We are using 3 budgetary ledgers for budget posting (Z1,Z2,Z3).Through budgeting workbench Z3 ledger consumes the Z2 ledger budget and Z2 ledger consumes the Z1 ledger budget. From Z3 ledger available balance the commitment and actual postings(PR/PO/IR) is triggering.

While transferring the budget from one budget address to another budget address system is not checking the sender budget and allowing to post the amount more than available amount. We have tried with FM budget consistency check and it is not working as expected.

Your help will be deeply appreciated.

Regards,

Syed Saleem Asifan.

entry.title: SAP Ariba Rest APIs: Gain a comprehensive understanding of rate limits!
entry.content:
SAP Ariba offers various REST APIs that enables developers to integrate their applications with SAP Ariba's robust procurement and supply chain management capabilities. In this article, we will delve into a crucial aspect of utilizing SAP Ariba REST APIs: rate limits. We will explore what rate limits are, how to find them, and how to leverage them to develop in a scalable manner.

Understanding Rate Limits

Rate limits define the maximum number of API requests an application can make within a specified time frame, typically expressed as a number of requests per second, minute, hour, and day. These limits are crucial in ensuring fair usage and preventing abuse, as well as maintaining optimal performance for all customers of the SAP Ariba REST API gateway. By adhering to rate limits, developers can design scalable applications that efficiently interact with SAP Ariba's system, mitigating the risk of overwhelming server resources or negatively impacting user experience.

Discovering Rate Limits

To develop effectively using the SAP Ariba REST APIs, it is essential to comprehend the rate limits imposed by the platform. These limits are typically documented in SAP Ariba's API documentation, which provides detailed information on various endpoints, methods, and associated rate limits. SAP Ariba categorizes its API endpoints into different groups, such as Authentication, Master Data, Transactions and Reporting. Each category may have its rate limits, which can vary based on the API endpoint's complexity and resource requirements.

For example, Analytical Reporting – Job Submission API has rate limit as 1/second, 2/minute, 8/hour, and 40/day.

Another example - Document Approval API has rate limit as 300/second, 800/minute, 35000/hour

By understanding and considering these rate limits during the development process, developers can ensure their applications meet the necessary performance criteria while avoiding disruption due to exceeded limits.

Rate Limit Management

To effectively utilize rate limits, developers must implement proper rate limit management strategies. Here are some best practices to consider:

  1. Respect Rate Limits: Always respect the rate limits set by SAP Ariba. Excessive API requests can result in rate limit exceedances or degraded performance. By being mindful of the limits imposed, developers can maintain the integrity of their applications.
  2. Use pagination and filtering: SAP Ariba REST APIs often support pagination and filtering options to limit result sets and reduce the amount of data transferred. Utilize these options effectively to improve performance and minimize resource usage.
  3. Implement error handling: Handle errors gracefully by capturing and interpreting response codes and error messages from SAP Ariba REST APIs. Implement appropriate error-handling logic to handle failures and provide meaningful feedback to the user.
  4. Monitor API usage: To ensure your application stays within rate limits, it is important to monitor your API usage. The response of each API call includes headers that contain the available rate limits for the specific endpoint. For instance, refer to the screenshot below that displays the remaining rate limits for the Analytical Reporting API endpoint - Job Results.  To Learn more about these headers, visit SAP Ariba help documentation
  5. Stay up to date with API changes: Keep track of any updates or changes to the SAP Ariba API and evaluate their impact on your integration. Regularly review SAP Ariba's release notes and documentation to ensure compatibility with the latest REST API versions.
  6. Consider storing data centrally to meet multiple use cases: If your organization has the requirement to use the same data for multiple integrations, it is recommended to pull it from SAP Ariba once, updating a central DB, and using that DB to connect to each downstream application. This will allow for exponential scalability and mitigate any performance related concerns. For example, check out mission "Extract SAP Ariba Cloud Services data through standard APIs" in SAP Discovery Center that pulls business related procurement data from SAP Ariba and stores it in a central database in BTP! 
  7. Leverage SAP API Management for Proxification, Governance, and Traffic Management and Monitoring. https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/api-management-capability

Conclusion: Overall, SAP Ariba open API enables businesses to extend the functionality of SAP Ariba and integrate it with other systems, fostering collaboration, automating processes, and enhancing procurement efficiency.

entry.title: Add attribute Account assignment group by customer like field on market segment report
entry.content:
Hi,

I would need to add Account assignment group by customer as a field in the market segment report so that I can be able to filter the data based on this characteristic.

I can add  Account assignment group by customer field as attribute from sales order field, but in this way i can't use this information to filter data.

 

Regards

entry.title: ChainVerifier: EC signed SHA256withRSA server certificate server certificate not capable for..
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am facing the following issue with establishing a connection using a RestReceiver. Unfortunately, there is an issue with the certificate. The following error is appearing:

ChainVerifier: EC signed SHA256withRSA server certificate server certificate not capable for ECDHE_ECDSA key exchange algorithm!

ChainVerifier: EC signed SHA256withRSA server certificate server certificate not capable for ECDHE_ECDSA key exchange algorithm!

There is an SAP note regarding this matter. It explains:

"Contact the administrators of the target server to create a certificate signed with the ECDSA encryption algorithm." 

3057117 - Peer-Zertifikat von ChainVerifier abgelehnt - EC-signiertes SHA256withRSA-Serverzertifikat Serverzertifikat nicht für ECDHE_ECDSA-Schlüsselaustauschalgorithmus geeignet! - SAP for Me

However, are there any other solutions available?

On the internet, it is mentioned that it is indeed possible to use a SHA256withRSA signature with the ECDHE_ECDSA key exchange algorithm.

Do you have any insights?
I would greatly appreciate your input.

Best Regards,
Mohammad

entry.title: Re: Unified Analytics with SAP Datasphere & Databricks Lakehouse Platform- Data Federation Scenarios
entry.content:
Hi  @marcelo_bruno  thanks for your feedback. I still see this as error with SQL sent to Databricks. Did you get additional logs? I would go with what @yaw_mak2  said about the parameter UseNativeQuery = 0.  This means "The connector transforms the queries emitted by applications and converts
them into an equivalent form in HiveQL". Did you already use it in your JDBC URL?

entry.title: BTP Trial Account - Cannot access ABAP Cloud project via ADT after SAP Build Booster
entry.content:
Hi, 

In my BTP trial account, I subscribed for SAP Build through booster and now I cannot log into my account using Eclipse. I am getting following error message - 

Error while logging on to the service instance using this project. Please make sure that you are logging on with <user_email> (<id>). 

I am searching online but I cannot understand why this is happening. Any help is appreciated.

Regards

ASM

entry.title: Re: Upload CSV to CRM Calendar
entry.content: As for your specific scenario, here's a general roadmap you could follow: Data Formatting: Ensure your Excel file is properly formatted with columns for dates and meeting details. Conversion to iCal: Use a script or tool to convert your Excel data into iCal format. This step might require some scripting or finding a suitable tool online. Upload Functionality: Implement a feature in your CRM Web UI to allow users to upload their iCal files.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Stephanie,

Thank you for the warm welcome. I will surely reach out to you and the other awesome individuals you mentioned as well. 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Thank you Stephanie.  I think Matt is the one who gave me access.  Still meeting new people all the time. 🙂

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
David,

Hello,

I'm a fresh face in the SAP community, having recently concluded a 22-year tenure with the U.S. Army. Transitioning into civilian life, I'm enthusiastic about embarking on a new career path within the realm of SAP. I've observed numerous colleagues make strides in their careers after engaging with SAP programs, and I'm eager to follow suit. With a background in Engineering and Military Intelligence, I recognize the significance of SAP and its contributions to the DoD enterprise. I'm keen to contribute to and be a part of this dynamic environment. I am interested in participating in the digital skills initiative. 

Kind regards,

Jamel J

Hi, did you find a solution? we are in the same situation for the Normal clerk as well as the special clerk. 

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Transport cash desk FQ_FPCJ_NC (Normal Clerk)
entry.content:
Hi, did you find a solution? we are in the same situation for the Normal clerk as well as the special clerk and we do not have any HR structure on the project. We  opened a ticket with still have not receive a solution to make these changes without a tranport request.

Regards.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: RSL issue in MB5M
entry.content: Hello Arjuna, I am having the same issue on a part that i set the total SL for and let SAP calculate the actual SLED. Even though i am manually entering most of the SLEDs, i still need the rounding rule se to the end of the month due to an evaluation check be active at the point of shipping. Without this it blocks orders from posting as soon as the RSL month hits. But we can still ship the material up until the end of the RSL month.

entry.title: Re: RAP vs CAP - Key Differences between the Two Programming Models
entry.content:
Hi @Raja - Came across this blog and thought of asking you the below question:-

  • Since BTP offers ABAP Environment(for RAP), CAP and also BAS for building Fiori, what is the recommended approach in developemnt for a customer who is on BTP?

entry.title: SAPCC 2205.0: Java Heapspace error while removing member from usergroup
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have 300k members in b2busergroup. when I try to remove a member from the group using DefaultB2BCommerceB2BUserGroupService.removeMemberFromUserGroup(), I am getting out of memory error. The below code is not written effectively to handle such a huge number of members. 

 

protected void removeMemberFromUserGroup(final B2BUserGroupModel usergroup, final B2BCustomerModel user)
{
	final HashSet<PrincipalModel> members = new HashSet<>(usergroup.getMembers());
	members.remove(user);
	usergroup.setMembers(members);
}

 

my analysis shows that if the user that I need to remove it in the last positions in the members set, then members.remove(user) is taking a longer time, because internally it queries the user table too many times. For example, if the user to be removed is at the 250000th position then I can see select * from users where PK = ? query getting executed 250,000 times. 

Is there a better way to Add/remove members into a user group?

Thanks,

Jobin

entry.title: Copy ST03N data with program in the SAP note 2568739 failed with error "Error in Control Framework"
entry.content:
We applied the note 2568739 as well as the Zreport in the note, however when running the program in the background job mode it failed with error:

Job started   
Step 001 started (program ZTMW_RC_MANAGE_ST03N_DATA, variant &0000000000000, user ID xxx ) 
Object ZTMW_RC_MANAGE_ST03N_DATA of class RE and language EN does not exist.   
Error in Control Framework      FES          022          E
Job canceled after system exception ERROR_MESSAGE   


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Copy ST03N data with program in the SAP note 2568739...
entry.content:
SAP Gui function GUI_UPLOAD/GUI_DOWNLOAD will be called in the program and lead to this error.
It is not allowed to run the report in background job mode, please run it in dialog mode.

Please refer to the KB 0003263260 Error in Control Framework Message no. FES022 when downloading or uploading data from SAP GUI through GUI_UPLOAD/GUI_DOWNLOAD Function Modules

entry.title: Re: MRP created multiple PR For Material Which Has Existi...
entry.content:
Hi Caetano,

System is not showing any exception message for PO rescheduling. How I can check that if the PO is reschedul or within rescheduling horizon.

entry.title: Re: Asset down payment acquisition reversal issue
entry.content: User doesn't want to reverse depreciation but want to reverse advance payment without affecting bank account.Since payment was withdrawn from their account.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Adapter Integration
entry.content: I cannot tell you how much I really appreciate this blog post!  I had been working on reading lists from SharePoint 365 over graph for months!  This new adapter works and I can return a SharePoint list.  Thank you for taking the time to write this!

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 3
Re: Environment, Health and Safety in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402
entry.content:
This is a bit late, but just to put a cap on a great post, here is the formal 2402 release announcement for SAP EHS

SAP EHS 2402 Release Announcement

entry.title: SFG Batch To FG Yield
entry.content:
Hello,

Lets say for example, a SFG PO has 6 batches ( 6 vats). Each batch is unique. When packing the SFG Batches against the FG PO, the FG PO will have only one batch. I'm wondering if there is a way SAP could show the FG yield for each individual SFG Batch. I can run SQL report for ea's produced per vat get yield per vat via MES data. However, I'm looking for a report a way this could be accomplished in SAP.  Documented goods movement visible via COR 3 for the SFG PO will show the volume used for each SFG Batch, however the FG PO's documented goods movement will only show the ea's for each HU produced all in a row.

any suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

thanks !

entry.title: Re: Service Layer - Linking Stages on Marketing Documents
entry.content:
Hi costas_ioannou2,

Unfortunately, this option is not available via DI or Service Layer as of now.

SAP Business One uses the Customer Influence site https://influence.sap.com/go/SAPBusinessOne for collecting, tracking, and prioritizing of requirements.

Please see and follow the recommendations in SAP Note 1028874, section B to post your requirement in SAP Business One Customer Influence site.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: What to do when you forget to click 'Without Qty Posting' on a credit?
entry.content: What is the easiest way to rectify this omission?  We didn't want to return the stock, only wanted to provide a partial credit for an incorrect price.

entry.title: How to set logic validation, when supplier invoice is reverse in the app 'Supplier invoice list'
entry.content:
We would like to set validation logic to check value of custom table when we reverse supplier invoice. 

We found there is not app for manage validation for supplier invoice reverse and could not find extension point id(BAdI) for it.

How to set logic validation, when supplier invoice is reverse in the app 'Supplier invoice list'?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to set logic validation, when supplier invoice is...
entry.content:
BADI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD can be used to perform customer specific checks of header and item data when creating supplier invoice. When you reverse a supplier invoice it also can be called and perform custom check.

But please note when you create supplier invoice the BADI is also triggered to ensuring that the invoice created will always meet your validation criteria. So I understand there should be no need to check it again when reversing invoice.

You can refer to this online documentation about all available BADI in supplier invoice for your information:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/5a27c5f57c3f4dffa6881ca587b22571.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: AI-related projects in your company
entry.content:
Hi There,

We are doing some exploration for SAP AI, there are some function can be realized like generate a simple report by typing in chatbox to describe. 

Also can do some knowledge search according to the files we upload.

but some customizing program it's impossible to read/change....so this is not very useful for senior consultant or in complex scenario

entry.title: Managing SCORM access with limited licenses
entry.content:
Due to some legacy licensing framework, a handful of our SCORM objects are on a usage/ license model. i.e. our organization is licensed to use a SCORM object for up to X number of course completions. Usage exceeding that will require additional fees to the content creator.

Currently, I can only think of scheduling reports for such item on regular basis to monitor the usage level.

I wonder if there is a more automatied/ sustainable way of managing? For example, is there a function in LMS that can manage the number of course completions for an Item (Online) containing such SCORM objects? 

SAP SuccessFactors Learning

entry.title: Re: Connector Builder Guide – Authentication Type: Custom
entry.content: Thanks, This is helpful to me.
